title: coreopsis petrophiloides
intro: Coreopsis petrophiloides, also known as the rock coreopsis, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Asteraceae family, native to the southeastern United States. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent qualities, which can help soothe wounds and reduce swelling. The health benefits of Coreopsis petrophiloides include its potential to aid in the treatment of skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as its ability to act as an antiseptic to prevent infection. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves and flowers can be used fresh or dried for medicinal purposes.
